what_it_does: Exposes a live, DOM-scoped catalog of spotlightable UI targets as window.surfacePointables(): it filters a hand-curated list of controls/legend by current DOM presence and auto-discovers additional targets from elements declaring data-ui-ref and data-point-label, returning opaque tokens plus human labels while keeping the ui:// addresses internal to the surface.
observations: Defines a curated CATALOG of ten pointables with token, ui:// address, and human label.
Filters the curated catalog by whether document.querySelector(`[data-ui-ref="..."]`) finds the element in the current DOM.
Auto-discovers pointables from any element with both data-ui-ref and data-point-label attributes.
Assigns opaque per-call auto-N tokens to auto-discovered targets so the ui:// address never reaches the brain.
Provides a small cssEscape helper that only escapes double quotes and backslashes for attribute selectors.
Comments flag labels as AI-supplied drafts awaiting Tim's final copy and note that sectors are deferred because DNA suppresses their spotlight visual.
Curated entries win over auto-discovered entries when they share the same address.
standouts: Strict address-free-brain contract: the brain receives opaque tokens and labels, never parseable ui:// addresses.
Hybrid registry design combining a maintained catalog with runtime auto-discovery via data attributes.
Live DOM filtering means the returned set reflects only currently rendered targets.
conventions: Uses TypeScript module exports and a one-time window augmentation for runtime exposure.
Uses data attributes (data-ui-ref, data-point-label) as the source of truth for auto-discovered targets.
Relies on token→address indirection to keep brain context address-free.
Heavy inline commentary explaining architectural rationale and ownership boundaries.
concerns: cssEscape only handles quotes and backslashes, not the full CSS identifier escape set, so unusual ui:// characters could break selectors.
Auto-discovered tokens are regenerated per call (auto-N), requiring consumers to map token→address from the same returned list.
No validation that curated addresses actually correspond to the intended rendered controls.
Only the first auto-discovered element per address is kept; duplicates are silently dropped.
notes: This file is the surface-side source of truth for what the brain can point at, mixing a hard-coded control catalog with runtime auto-discovery.
questions: Which consumer reads window.surfacePointables() and how are per-call auto tokens kept in sync with the token→address map?
What is the full set of characters that can appear in ui:// addresses, and is cssEscape sufficient for them?
How will sector pointables be integrated once DNA un-suppresses their spotlight visual?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — a surface-sourced, address-free, live catalog of pointable controls — matches the implementation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to let the brain reference UI controls by human meaning without leaking implementation addresses, while allowing new surface components to become spotlightable without editing a central registry.
novelty: The combination of a curated, hand-labeled control catalog with an auto-discovery path that generates opaque per-call tokens from inline data attributes, enforcing an address-free contract for the brain.
contribution: A runtime pointability registry that lets the surface declare which rendered elements are spotlightable and provides the brain with opaque tokens and human labels, keeping ui:// addresses internal to the surface.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript module that installs window.surfacePointables() to return a live, tokenized catalog of spotlightable UI controls and auto-discovered elements, mapping opaque tokens to ui:// addresses without exposing addresses to the brain.
intention_for_embedding: To let the brain point at surface controls by meaning rather than address, using a hybrid curated-plus-discovered registry that reflects the currently rendered DOM.

symbols.does: Declares the shape of a pointable target: opaque token, ui:// address, and human label.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type used for both curated catalog entries and auto-discovered elements, ensuring every pointable exposes an opaque token, a concrete address, and a human-readable description.

symbols.does: Escapes double quotes and backslashes so a ui:// address can be safely embedded in a CSS attribute selector.
symbols.description: A minimal helper used when querying `[data-ui-ref="..."]` for curated catalog entries; it only handles quote and backslash characters, leaving other CSS special characters unescaped.

symbols.does: Installs window.surfacePointables() once, returning the live set of currently rendered pointable targets.
symbols.description: The exported installer attaches a global function that filters the curated CATALOG against the DOM, auto-discovers elements carrying data-ui-ref and data-point-label, assigns opaque auto-N tokens, and returns the merged list so the brain can point at controls without receiving addresses.
